skill.name: postmortem-writing
skill.description: Guides teams through blameless incident postmortems with timelines, root cause analysis, lessons learned, and action items.
skill.summary: Create structured, blameless postmortems that turn incidents into clear learning and prevention work.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they occur inside Markdown examples, prose, and incident templates rather than executable code. The only confirmed risk factor is low-risk network exposure from three external reference links; no prompt injection, malicious intent, credential access, or runnable command execution was found.

low_findings.title: External Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The skill links to Google SRE, Etsy, and PagerDuty postmortem resources. These are user-visible references, not hidden network requests or data exfiltration paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are explicit Markdown resource links at the end of the guide. There is no code that fetches them or sends user data to them.

low_findings.title: Static Command-Execution Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby and shell backtick locations are fenced Markdown examples, inline code formatting, diagrams, and templates. SKILL.md contains guidance text only and no executable script blocks that run commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are prose or Markdown template content. The file contains no Ruby, shell, JavaScript, or Python execution logic around these lines.

low_findings.title: Static Blocker Findings Are Prose False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography, at scheduling, and system reconnaissance findings are triggered by ordinary incident-response wording and template content. No cryptographic operation, task scheduling command, or host reconnaissance command is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are YAML metadata, postmortem analysis prompts, or best-practice prose. I found no executable commands or security-sensitive implementation in those contexts.

low_findings.title: Network Library Finding Is Textual False Positive
low_findings.description: The Python HTTP library finding at line 229 is caused by the word requests in an incident evidence sentence. It does not import a library or perform any HTTP operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 229 is inside a 5 Whys postmortem example and describes pending requests as operational evidence. There is no Python code or network call at this location.

content.value_statement: Incident reviews often become inconsistent, blame-focused, or hard to act on. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code draft structured postmortems with clear timelines, root causes, and owners.
actual_capabilities: Drafts standard postmortem documents with summary, impact, timeline, root cause, detection, response, and action items.
Provides a 5 Whys analysis format for identifying systemic causes and prevention work.
Creates quick postmortems for lower-severity incidents with concise timelines and fixes.
Guides facilitation of postmortem meetings with agenda structure and discussion prompts.
Lists practical best practices and anti-patterns for blameless incident reviews.
limitations: Does not collect logs, metrics, chat history, or ticket data automatically.
Requires accurate incident details from the user to produce a reliable postmortem.
Does not replace security, legal, compliance, or executive approval for sensitive incidents.
External resource links are references and may require internet access to open.

use_cases.title: Draft a SEV1 or SEV2 Postmortem
use_cases.description: Turn incident notes into a structured review with impact, timeline, root cause, lessons, and assigned follow-up work.

use_cases.title: Run a Blameless Review Meeting
use_cases.description: Use the facilitation guide to keep discussion focused on system conditions, evidence, and concrete improvements.

use_cases.title: Analyze Recurring Incident Patterns
use_cases.description: Apply the 5 Whys format to compare incidents and identify missing tests, documentation gaps, or alerting weaknesses.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Postmortem
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to draft a blameless postmortem from these incident notes: [paste notes]. Include summary, impact, timeline, root cause, and action items.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Timeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to convert these alerts, chat updates, and deployment events into a UTC incident timeline: [paste events]. Flag missing or unclear timestamps.

prompt_templates.title: Run 5 Whys Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to perform a blameless 5 Whys analysis for this incident: [describe incident]. Separate proximate causes from systemic improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Action Items
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review this draft postmortem: [paste draft]. Improve weak action items, remove blame language, and identify gaps in evidence or ownership.

output_examples.input: Raw notes from a payment outage with alerts, rollback time, impact numbers, and suspected database connection exhaustion.
output_examples.output: A postmortem draft with an executive summary, customer and business impact, chronological timeline, root cause analysis, lessons learned, and prioritized action items.

output_examples.input: A short SEV3 latency incident with five timestamps and one known mitigation.
output_examples.output: A concise quick postmortem covering what happened, when it happened, root cause, immediate fix, and one long-term prevention task.

output_examples.input: A completed postmortem draft that includes blame language and vague follow-up tasks.
output_examples.output: A revised review that uses neutral language, focuses on system conditions, and turns broad recommendations into owned, measurable actions.

best_practices: Provide exact times, impact numbers, detection signals, and remediation steps before asking for a final draft.
Keep the review blameless by focusing on system conditions, process gaps, and missing safety nets.
Convert every prevention idea into an owned action item with priority, due date, and tracking reference.
anti_patterns: Using the skill to assign personal blame or create disciplinary narratives.
Publishing a postmortem without validating facts, timelines, and customer impact with incident responders.
Accepting vague action items that lack an owner, due date, or measurable completion criteria.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create blameless postmortems, 5 Whys analyses, quick incident reviews, timelines, and action item tables.

faq.question: Does it gather incident data automatically?
faq.answer: No. You must provide notes, logs, timestamps, metrics, or draft text for the model to organize and improve.

faq.question: Can it handle minor incidents?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a quick postmortem format for lower-severity incidents that still need learning and follow-up.

faq.question: How does it support blameless culture?
faq.answer: It redirects analysis toward system conditions, missing safeguards, detection gaps, and process improvements.

faq.question: Can it be used with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. It is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code for drafting and reviewing incident documentation.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive incidents?
faq.answer: Use normal data handling controls. Remove secrets and private customer data before sharing incident notes with any AI tool.
